name: write_then_read_returns_written

name: read_uncontained_key_arbitrary
code:
proof fn phi_3_read_uncontained_key_arbitrary(pt1: PTMem, pt2: PTMem, addr: usize)
    requires
        !pt1.mem.contains_key(addr),
        !pt2.mem.contains_key(addr),
    ensures
        pt1.read(addr) == pt2.read(addr),
{
}
reason: Reading an address not in the map domain returns the same `arbitrary()` value for any two PTMem instances — `Map::index` on missing keys returns a deterministic but unspecified value per key

name: write_to_pml4_addr_no_effect_on_field
code:
proof fn phi_5_write_to_pml4_addr_no_effect_on_field(pt: PTMem, value: usize)
    requires
        value != pt.pml4,
    ensures
        pt.write(pt.pml4, value).pml4 == pt.pml4,
        pt.write(pt.pml4, value).read(pt.pml4) == value,
{
}
reason: Writing a different value to the address equal to `pml4` changes memory but not the `pml4` field — the PML4 base pointer diverges from what's stored at its own address
